behaviors.description: The generated function signature matches the expected interface (parameter names, types, return type) specified in the description.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is model judgment on interface extraction from natural language. A stubbed model can only confirm parser/validator wiring, not whether real generations preserve names/types/return. Rejected lower level (Integration): behavior text targets generated signature correctness itself, which depends on real LLM output content.
behaviors.test_description: Setup: send a real prompt with a strict expected signature to the live model path; Assertion: parsed signature exactly matches expected names/types/return; Catches: real model emits plausible but wrong signature that deterministic stubs would miss.

behaviors.description: Orchestration executes attempts in order and returns immediately on the first passing attempt.
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration/state handoff across local modules (generator client, test runner adapter, attempt controller). No real OS/network/model-quality dependence is required if generator/test results are deterministically stubbed. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is control flow ('in order', 'returns immediately'), not LLM output quality or multi-agentic decisioning.
behaviors.test_description: Setup: stub attempt-1 as fail and attempt-2 as pass with call tracing; Assertion: attempt-3 never runs and recorded order is 1->2; Catches: controller continues after first pass or misorders retries.

behaviors.description: For a function that a single call usually fails, the live iterative loop can still produce a passing implementation within the allowed attempts.
behaviors.justification: Requires multiple real agentic steps in sequence (initial generation, failure-informed refinement(s), eventual pass). A single live call test cannot expose the iterative recovery behavior. Rejected lower level (Agentic): behavior explicitly requires loop success across attempts, not one model decision.
behaviors.test_description: Setup: run end-to-end live loop on a benchmark prompt known to often fail first attempt; Assertion: within <=3 attempts one attempt passes full tests; Catches: retry/refinement chain fails to improve despite valid iterative design intent.

behaviors.description: When tests fail, the refinement prompt builder includes the failed test names and failure messages in the next LLM prompt.
behaviors.justification: Failure occurs at module boundary: test-result collector -> prompt builder payload. Real model output is not needed; inspect constructed next prompt. Integration brake: no required OS/network/subprocess, no model-quality dependence, no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Setup: feed orchestrator a failed test report fixture with names/messages; Assertion: next prompt contains those exact failures in structured form; Catches: dropped/truncated failure context during handoff.

behaviors.description: The next live attempt changes implementation content in response to prior failure context rather than resubmitting the same code.
behaviors.justification: Needs at least two real agentic generations with state carryover (fail context -> revised code). Mocked model responses cannot validate genuine responsiveness to context. Rejected lower level (Agentic): behavior text requires sequenced comparison between attempts, i.e., multi-step agentic loop.
behaviors.test_description: Setup: execute live attempt-1 and attempt-2 with real failure feedback injected from attempt-1 tests; Assertion: attempt-2 code is materially different and targets cited failure; Catches: loop resubmits near-identical code despite refinement context.

behaviors.description: The service stops after 3 failed attempts and returns a structured error with all three attempts and their test results.
behaviors.justification: Primarily retry-limit enforcement plus attempt bookkeeping/serialization across modules. Deterministic stubs for generator/test outcomes are sufficient to trigger three failures and verify payload. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is termination/reporting logic, not model-quality or real infra behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Setup: stub three consecutive failing attempts with distinct test-result payloads; Assertion: service returns error after attempt 3 containing exactly three attempt records; Catches: off-by-one retries or incomplete error structure.

behaviors.description: The full non-agentic pipeline wiring (generate call, test execution, attempt bookkeeping) works end to end with deterministic stubbed model outputs.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly scopes to non-agentic deterministic wiring; target failures are module integration defects. Rejected higher levels: text disqualifies Agentic/Workflow ('non-agentic', 'stubbed model outputs'); System only needed if asserting real subprocess/OS behavior, which this behavior does not require.
behaviors.test_description: Setup: run end-to-end pipeline with stub generator outputs across pass/fail cases and deterministic test executor fixture; Assertion: generate->execute->bookkeep transitions and final status are correct; Catches: broken wiring between pipeline stages under realistic module composition.

behaviors.description: The full live loop (generate, test, refine, retest) solves at least one known hard function that fails in the first attempt.
behaviors.justification: Explicitly a full multi-step live agentic pipeline with iterative improvement requirement. Real model behavior across chained steps is the failure boundary. Rejected lower levels: behavior text requires live generate+refine+retest sequence and first-attempt failure then recovery.
behaviors.test_description: Setup: run live loop on a curated hard task with expected first-attempt failure profile; Assertion: first attempt fails and a later attempt within limit passes; Catches: real-world iterative pipeline cannot recover on challenging tasks despite nominal retry logic.
